completed_tasks.name: discuss-phase: gather Phase 8 context (chain mode, wall tool, show/hide, crosshair)
STATE.md session record for Phase 8 context

remaining_tasks.name: /gsd-execute-phase 8 — implement plans (chain mode, wall tool, show/hide toggle, crosshair cursor)
Phase 8 closure — update ROADMAP.md / REQUIREMENTS.md / STATE.md

decisions.decision: Chain mode break trigger = Esc OR re-click active tool button (tool-switch implicitly breaks)
decisions.rationale: Reuses existing toolbar toggle affordance the user already knows

decisions.decision: Chain mode visual = toolbar active style + name+color chip badge near button
decisions.rationale: Indicator stays close to the affordance; not noisy on dense plans

decisions.decision: Chain persists across PDF page navigation (does NOT auto-break on page change)
decisions.rationale: Estimators place the same item across multiple pages — page-change break would defeat the productivity goal

decisions.decision: Chain state lives inside useMarkupTool (renderer hook), not in store / .clmc / localStorage
decisions.rationale: Chain is runtime UX state only; does not survive app restart

decisions.decision: Wall = new 'wall' MarkupType (separate shape) with points + wallHeight (mm)
decisions.rationale: Cleanest discriminated-union extension; aggregator branches on type, not on a field

decisions.decision: Wall height: per-wall popup input on commit; chain inherits previous wall height; first wall default = 2400mm
decisions.rationale: Fits the chain pattern exactly; 2400mm = typical residential ceiling

decisions.decision: Wall popup = MarkupNamePopup with conditional 'Wall height: [___] mm' row when toolType === 'wall'
decisions.rationale: One popup component handles all five markup tools; minimum new UI surface

decisions.decision: Wall BOQ representation = single row per name in m² (length × height); UoM = 'm²'; matches area markup row shape
decisions.rationale: Matches how walls are billed; estimator-friendly; no novel multi-row pattern

decisions.decision: Show/hide visibility = per-project (.clmc additive field 'hiddenItemNames: string[]'), additive non-breaking schema bump
decisions.rationale: Visibility is takeoff state (estimator's intentional working view), not a workstation preference like collapsedCategories

decisions.decision: Visibility icon = lucide Lightbulb / LightbulbOff, left of color chip in TotalsRow, always visible
decisions.rationale: User explicit preference over Eye/EyeOff — lightbulb metaphor reads as 'turn off the light over this item'

decisions.decision: Hidden behavior = canvas markups skip render; row stays interactive; totals & BOQ export INCLUDE hidden items
decisions.rationale: Pure visual decluttering; totals are the source of truth; export filtering deferred
View preference, not a takeoff action; flips a project-store field directly and marks dirty

decisions.decision: Crosshair = rifle-scope style (1px crossed lines + circular gap at center, no dot, white with thin black outline)
decisions.rationale: User explicit preference; the empty center reveals the underlying pixel exactly without obscuring it

decisions.decision: Crosshair implementation = CSS cursor with SVG data-URL on CanvasViewport containerRef
decisions.rationale: Zero render cost, no Konva node, no mousemove listener; OS handles hide/restore over chrome automatically

decisions.decision: Crosshair scope = all markup tools (count/linear/area/perimeter/wall) + Set Scale + Verify Scale
decisions.rationale: Any canvas-pointing tool benefits from precision pointing

next_action: /clear then /gsd-plan-phase 8 — read .planning/phases/08-markup-workflow-and-wall-tool/08-CONTEXT.md and produce PLAN.md(s) with task breakdown for the four features (chain mode, wall tool, show/hide, crosshair).
context_notes: Phase 8 context fully captured — 4 areas discussed (chain mode, wall tool, show/hide, crosshair), 20 numbered decisions in 08-CONTEXT.md, full audit trail in 08-DISCUSSION-LOG.md. No SPEC.md was created (discuss-phase only). Researcher should expect to investigate: (1) wall renderer affordance options (subtle stroke vs parallel offset hairline vs hatching), (2) wall toolbar lucide icon choice, (3) EditMarkupCommand wall-height extension shape (extend existing 'edit-markup' branch with optional oldHeight/newHeight, or sibling 'edit-wall' branch), (4) project-schema additive field migration policy (formatVersion bump vs per-field defaulting), (5) CSS cursor data-URL hotspot coords + Chromium 32x32 max image-size constraint. The chain mode design is the highest-leverage item — it touches all five markup tools and the wall tool reuses it. Hidden-item rendering must be wired into all five renderer components AND HoverRing/PulseHighlight.
